title: Age group badge
description: Compact chip showing `<name> · <bounds>` (e.g. `U12 · Ages 10-11`, `Adult · Ages 18-34`). Rendered in team detail + roster rows + enrollment previews + list-view age-group column. The chip's background color is driven by AgeGroup.color; when the group is is_youth=true, a subscript 'youth' chip renders in the safeguarding-yellow variant so the surrounding UI signals the minor-scoped downstream consequences.
notes: Uses HeroUI `Chip` — no bespoke class-name literals per the ui-components rule.
The primary chip's background color comes from AgeGroup.color (hex). When color is null, falls back to a neutral variant.
The subscript 'youth' chip renders as `Chip variant='warning' size='xs'` — the tenant's standard youth-safeguarding accent.
The bounds summary is locale-neutral (numeric only). The 'Ages' prefix is i18n-keyed for RTL locales that render age ranges right-to-left.
When `showSportKey=true` AND the AgeGroup has a non-null sport_key, appends a third chip with the sport identifier (`football`, `basketball`, …).

isYouth.description: Renders the subscript 'youth' chip in safeguarding-yellow when true.

color.description: Hex color for the primary chip's background (e.g. `#3B82F6`). Nullable falls back to the neutral variant.

sportKey.description: Optional sport identifier (`football`, `basketball`, …). Renders a third chip when set + showSportKey=true.

showSportKey.description: When false, hides the sport_key chip even when the value is present.

isDefault.description: When true, renders a subscript 'default' chip (leading star icon).

showBounds.description: When false, renders just the name (compact tables + roster rows where the bounds live in a separate column).
